title: T-045 — Inventory-value RBAC server-side gating (WH §14.5)
prompt:
# T-045 — Inventory-value RBAC server-side gating (WH §14.5)

PRD: docs/prd/05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md §14.5
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Implement server-side gating on the inventory-value column: only roles in (Manager, Planner, Admin) see SUM(LP.qty × items.cost) values; other roles get null/omitted columns (V-WH-DASH-001).

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/inventory-value.ts`: `getInventoryValue({warehouse_id, user_role}): {value: Decimal|null, currency, suppressed: bool}`.
2. If role ∉ ('Manager','Planner','Admin') → `{value: null, suppressed: true}` (no SUM computed, value never leaves DB).
3. Inventory Browser RSC (T-053) calls this for the value column; never include cost in responses to unauthorized roles.
4. Caches per-tenant Redis 60s (settings.dashboard_cache_ttl_sec).

## Files
- apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/inventory-value-rbac.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/__tests__/inventory-value-rbac.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a Manager calls the API, when invoked, then `value` is a Decimal sum across active LPs.
2. Given an Operator calls the API, when invoked, then `value=null, suppressed=true` and the actual SUM is never computed.
3. Given the cache key is hit within 60 s, when invoked, then DB query is NOT re-executed.
4. Given the role gate evaluates server-side, when a client tampers with role param, then the server uses the session role and ignores it.

## Test strategy
- RED: `apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/__tests__/inventory-value.test.ts` asserting role gate, suppression, cache, server-side trust.
- Run `pnpm --filter web test inventory-value`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not compute SUM for unauthorized roles (data minimization).
- Do not trust client-side role hints.
- Do not cache cross-tenant.
